description: Capability classes — the bounded, scale-invariant set of NEED KINDS the router classifies a query into BEFORE picking an agent. This set does NOT grow with the agent count: 5 agents or 500, a query is still one of these kinds. Each agent declares which kind(s) it serves via `capabilities` on its card; the router admits only agents whose capabilities include the classified kind. `priority` breaks a CROSS-KIND tie deterministically (higher wins) so a genuinely ambiguous query resolves the same way every time. `principle` is a semantic definition (NOT a keyword list, per §2.1) used to classify a query into this kind.

capabilities.principle: the user wants the facts, status, fields, or timeline of ONE specific record they have identified (by id or active focus)
the user wants to OBTAIN, order, provision, request, or get access to something new
the user wants an ANSWER, how-to, explanation, or fix — including a bare problem or symptom stated with no other ask; the safe default when intent is unclear
the user wants to triage, classify, prioritise, assign, or otherwise MODIFY a record
the user explicitly wants a SET of existing records handed back that resemble a described problem or a named record — the records themselves are the deliverable, not an answer or a fix
